name: Chicken Breasts with Eggplant and Roasted Red Pepper Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup red wine vinegar, or white
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the fresh herbs: finely chop 1 to 2 tablespoons of fresh herbs such as thyme or parsley.
2. Roast the peppers: Place the peppers on the grill or under the broiler, turning them occasionally until charred all over and nearly black. Remove from heat and place in a plastic bag to steam off the skin. Once cool enough to handle, remove the charred skins and seeds.
3. Preheat the grill or broiler. Halve the eggplant and score each half 2 or 3 times. Brush with some of the olive oil and place on the grill, cut side toward the heat. After 5 minutes, turn and cook until nearly soft.
4. Add the chicken breasts to the pan and cook for an additional 3 minutes on each side, or until fully cooked. Remove the chicken to a plate.
5. Heat the remaining olive oil in a large frying pan until hot. Add the onion and garlic, cooking until softened.
6. Puree the roasted peppers and vinegar in a food processor or blender until smooth.
7. Return the chicken mixture to the pan with the onions, garlic, and purée. Cook together for about 5 minutes.
8. Arrange the chicken mixture on a platter surrounded by the eggplant slices and sauce. Sprinkle with the fresh herbs.
